items.title: Social infrastructure during heatwave: 'Knock on the door makes the difference between life & death'
items.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome Eric Klinenberg, Professor of Sociology at NYU and Author of "Heat Wave: A Social Autopsy of Disaster in Chicago". He challenges one of the dominant assumptions surrounding extreme heat: that technological adaptation alone can protect societies from climate change. Drawing on his landmark study of Chicago's deadly 1995 heatwave, he argues that mortality during extreme heat is shaped as much by the strength of social ties as by meteorological conditions.

'Big polluting companies like Total have climate obligations under French law'
items.description: Genie Godula is pleased to welcome Justine Ripoll, Campaign Manager for French NGO "Notre Affaire à Tous", and one of four NGOs who brought the climate case against Total Energy. She argues that climate litigation not as a symbolic confrontation with the fossil fuel industry, but as an evolving mechanism of governance that seeks to clarify and enforce corporate responsibility under climate law. Ripoll shifts the conversation from technological inevitability to political and ethical prioritisation, arguing that the remaining carbon budget should be allocated according to collective social needs rather than commercial interests. She asserts that multinational energy companies can no longer position climate change solely as a matter for governments. Instead, she contends that businesses whose activities substantially contribute to greenhouse gas emissions carry independent legal obligations that courts are increasingly willing to recognise.

items.description: Ukraine and its European allies are looking to encourage investment in Kyiv's post-war recovery. The first tranche of an EU loan has already arrived with more deals on the table. But relations with Poland are strained.

items.title: Russia denies pressuring Belarus to widen Ukraine conflict; Minsk blames West
items.description: The Wall Street Journal reported ‌that Russia wanted to use Belarus as a springboard to step up ⁠attacks on Ukraine, and that Moscow was threatening to cut financial support if it did not agree

items.title: Trump offers Iran an economic lifeline — with strings
items.description: Iran has seen a speedy rebound in oil exports after the US-Iran deal granted immediate waivers on US sanctions. Relief on frozen assets and a $300 billion reconstruction fund now hinge on Tehran meeting key conditions.

items.title: Paris neighbourhoods: The dynamic 10th arrondissement
items.description: In this edition of French Connections, Genie Godula and Florence Villeminot continue their tour of Paris with a stop in the 10th arrondissement of Paris. Located in the heart of the Right Bank, it’s one of the most vibrant parts of the capital. A lot of people enter Paris through the dixième, thanks to the city’s two biggest train stations, the Gare de Nord et Gare de l’Est. They, along with the much-loved Canal Saint Martin, have helped turn the 10th into a multicultural melting pot of restaurants, bars, theatres and other hidden gems that capture the essence of real Parisian life.

items.title: France confirms first ever case of Ebola in doctor returning from DRC
items.description: France on Wednesday announced its first confirmed case of Ebola identified on its territory, a doctor back from the Democratic Republic of the Congo, which is fighting a major outbreak.

items.title: Lionel Scaloni on Argentina's win over Austria: 'When Messi switches on, everyone switches on'
items.description: Lionel Messi scored both goals in Argentina's 2-0 victory over Austria, securing the Albiceleste's place in the knockout stages of the 2026 World Cup. Leer más

items.title: Milei shuns Wall Street to raise dollars at rate lower than 7%
items.description: Luis Caputo stuck to a strategy that many investors initially saw as too risky: secure dollars through cheaper alternative channels, buy time and wait for spreads to come down. Leer más

items.title: Independent MPs launch new Australian centrist party
items.description: Zali Steggall and Allegra Spender say Community Strong Australia will offer citizens 'reason over rage'.

items.title: Court of Appeal postpones hearing in ADC, other parties’ deregistration appeal
items.description: The appeal stems from a Federal High Court judgement that directed the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) to deregister the five parties. The post Court of Appeal postpones hearing in ADC, other parties’ deregistration appeal appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Russia set to return to FIFA competition at inaugural U-15 World Cup
items.description: FIFA's inaugural U-15 World Cup in October is opened to all of its member associates, paving way for Russia's return.

items.title: Milei leads rightist leaders congratulating Colombian president-elect
items.description: Argentina's President Javier Milei one of the first to congratulate Colombia's hard-right president-elect Abelardo de la Espriella. Leer más

items.title: MSCI upgrades Bulgaria to frontier; Vietnam, Argentina unchanged

items.title: Stellantis, Nissan eye Marelli assets in rescue talks, Bloomberg reports
Right sweeps Latin America with new victory in Colombia
items.description: The right has won elections from Argentina to Honduras. But experts see little evidence of a structural ideological shift. Instead, they point to a political landscape that favours anti-establishment outsider candidates. Leer más

items.title: Minimum wage has lost 40% of its purchasing power under Milei
items.description: Basic income for registered workers has plunged against November 2023 to levels even inferior to the 2001 crisis, according to a report by UBA Buenos Aires University’s Economics Faculty. Economic growth produces winners but they are not a majority. Leer más

items.title: UN adopts resolution upholding accountability for attacks on peacekeepers
items.description: It authorises new steps to ensure that those who commit crimes against peacekeepers face justice.

items.title: Thousands feared dead in Venezuela quakes as rescuers search rubble
items.description: Jorge Rodriguez, who heads Venezuela's national assembly, says at least 188 people have died, 1,520 people have been injured, and at least 250 buildings have been damaged or destroyed.

items.title: Argentina’s record against next World Cup 2026 opponent Austria
items.description: Lionel Scaloni’s side will be looking to secure qualification for the Round of 32 in next group-stage clash against Austria; Teams have previously met just twice, with one win for Argentina and one draw. Leer más

items.title: Pope Leo XIV expected to visit Argentina in November on three-day stop
items.description: Argentine military bishop who met with pontiff at the Vatican this week says the itinerary is "practically defined" – though the Holy See has yet to issue an official announcement. Leer más

items.title: Strait of Hormuz: How months of closure creates  challenges for ships
items.description: Even if shipping traffic resumes through the Strait of Hormuz, hundreds of vessels that have spent weeks or months waiting at anchor must first overcome a range of technical, operational and human challenges before returning to normal trade

items.title: La Guaira, Venezuela’s port city, shows extensive earthquake damage after the quake
items.description: New images from La Guaira on 25 June revealed widespread destruction after twin earthquakes struck Venezuela's Caribbean coast, damaging buildings, roads and key public infrastructure.

items.title: Lionel Messi, Argentina's own answer to Peter Pan
items.description: With Lionel Messi, that X-factor one always needs to win tight games is never far away. Leer más

items.title: Scotland: Sturgeon's ex-husband Peter Murrell jailed for 5 years and 3 months
items.description: Peter Murrell pleaded guilty to embezzling funds in his role as chief executive of the Scottish National Party. He held the SNP's purse strings while his then-wife, Nicola Sturgeon, was Scottish First Minister.

items.title: Kenya to charge students for murder in deadly dormitory fire
items.description: Kenyan prosecutors said Tuesday they will charge a number of students with murder of 16 schoolmates in the burning of a dormitory in late May that shocked the east African country.

items.title: Why Austrian author Ingeborg Bachmann remains a literary icon
items.description: A new documentary featuring Sandra Hüller celebrates the Austrian poet and author who was a literary star of her era. She would have turned 100 this year.

items.title: Iraq urges OPEC to raise its quota
items.description: OPEC has "responded by launching a process to reassess" its members states' capacities, the Ministry said

items.title: Heat and drinks breaks help smaller teams at World Cup, says Argentina coach Scaloni
items.description: Hydration breaks are helping the smaller teams at the World Cup, Argentina coach Lionel Scaloni said Sunday. Leer más

items.title: World Cup 2026: More MENA countries competing than ever before
items.description: More Middle East and North Africa (MENA) countries are competing in the 2026 World Cup than at any previous tournament, much to the delight of fans from Morocco to Jordan. But as always with sports, politics is never far from the pitch. Iran, of course, springs to mind. Never before has a country hosted a World Cup while at war with one of the competing nations.

items.title: On the beach with migrants determined to cross Channel to UK
items.description: Sunny weather and calm seas have meant the number of migrants attempting the dangerous crossing from northern France to the UK in small boats has risen sharply in the last week. In April this year, the UK agreed to provide 767 million euros to France over the next three years to reinforce policing along the coastline. But migrants, when they arrive in northern France, have often already crossed several different borders and they are determined to make the crossing to what they believe will be a better life in the UK. Our reporters met with migrants on the beach in Gravelines near Calais.

items.title: The ICE raids are over, but some Minnesotans say they still live in fear
items.description: Months after the Trump administration's immigration crackdown officially ended, Minneapolis is still feeling the impact.

items.title: Poland, Ukraine hold annual reconstruction conference without Zelensky
items.description: A landmark conference on Ukraine's post-war reconstruction opened on Thursday in Poland without President Volodymyr Zelensky, who is snubbing the event over a diplomatic row with ally Warsaw and weeks of squabbling over historical differences. Kyiv sent Ukrainian Prime Minister Yulia Svyrydenko, who struck a friendly tone and didn't mention the historical disagreements.

items.title: Ghosn says calls for his Nissan return show investor anger over results
Zimbabwe Senate approves bill to extend presidential term
items.description: Zimbabwe's Senate on Wednesday overwhelmingly approved controversial constitutional amendments that would keep President Emmerson Mnangagwa in office until 2030, giving the changes full parliamentary support after earlier consent from the lower house

items.title: Ukraine says major Crimea bridge destroyed in latest attack
items.description: Ukraine continues to hit infrastructure on the Crimea to isolate the Russian-annexed peninsula. Meanwhile, Russia attacked Volodymyr Zelenskyy's hometown with a ballistic missile in a deadly strike.

items.title: Air conditioning creates political divide after France records hottest day
items.description: France is being forced to re-think its longstanding reservations about air-conditioning as temperatures soar.

items.title: Fresh dispute emerges over Strait of Hormuz transit routes despite ceasefire
items.description: Naval Force of IRGC warns vessels transiting the strait must use only routes officially designated by Iran

items.title: Argentina’s economy grew more than expected in first quarter
items.description: Gross domestic product expanded 0.7% compared to the previous quarter, above analyst forecasts, even as unemployment rose and wages trailed inflation. Leer más

items.title: 2 earthquakes in Venezuela kill at least 164. And, Trump cancels housing bill signing
items.description: Two major earthquakes in Venezuela have killed at least 164 people and left hundreds injured. And, President Trump canceled the signing of a massive bipartisan housing bill yesterday.

items.title: Paraguay's Australian-made commune nervous for crucial Cup clash
items.description: In rural Paraguay, a small farming town flies Australian flags. When Paraguay plays Australia at the 2026 World Cup today, its residents will be at the edge of their seats.

items.title: Trump airs grievances with NATO chief over alliance's lack of Iran war support
items.description: President Trump met with NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte Wednesday, where he shared his frustrations with the lack of support he's received from allies on the Iran war.

items.title: Tunisia upholds eight-year jail term for anti-racism activist
items.description: A Tunisian court on Tuesday upheld an eight-year prison sentence slapped on anti-racism activist Saadia Mosbah for financial misconduct, two of her lawyers said

items.title: Deconfliction and disarmament: Can both be pursued in Lebanon?
items.description: New deconfliction mechanism seeks to prevent return to all-out war in Lebanon and in the region.

items.title: What's on the agenda as Macron meets Meloni for rare summit?
items.description: Italy's Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni is set to meet French President Emmanuel Macron Thursday in a bid to tighten ties. Their relationship thus far has been marked by political differences, but Meloni's rift with US leader Donald Trump and shared interests are drawing the two leaders together. Emerald Maxwell takes a look
